---
title: "Module 03: Trees and Two Cultures"
---

## Learning objectives

- Contrast **data modeling** vs **algorithmic modeling** (Breiman) without вЂњstats vs MLвЂќ slogans.
- Explain **decision trees** as **ifвЂ“then** rules and rectangular regions in predictor space.
- Map Monday **`rpart`** controls to Tuesday **`parsnip` `decision_tree()`** knobs.

[в†ђ Module library](index.qmd) В· [Next: Module 04 вЂ” Pipeline в†’](module-04-pipeline.qmd)

---

## On the slides (Day 2)

| Block | Topic | Slide link |
|-------|--------|------------|
| Part A | Two cultures, `rpart` intro | [Part A](../slides/day-02-tuesday.html#/part-a-cultures) |
| Part B | Gene Г— disease steps, gene Г— trait `cp` | [Day 2 deck](../slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd) (includes) |
| Part C | `recipe` в†’ `spec` в†’ `workflow` в†’ tune (**trees only**) | [Part C pipeline](../slides/day-02-tuesday.html#/part-c-pipeline) |
| Part D | Transition to Thursday | [Wrap-up](../slides/day-02-tuesday.html#/part-d-wrapup) |

Canonical code: [`day02-tidymodels-walkthrough.qmd`](../_includes/day02-tidymodels-walkthrough.qmd) (also [Module 04](module-04-pipeline.qmd)).

---

## Two cultures (compressed)

- **Data modeling:** write down a stochastic model, emphasize parameters and assumptions.  
- **Algorithmic modeling:** flexible fit, judge by **prediction** on held-out data.  
- Both need **honest splits** and clear **leakage** rules.

---

## Tree mechanics

- **Greedy binary splits** on one predictor at a time.  
- Classification: improve **purity** (Gini); regression: reduce **within-node variance**.  
- **Depth**, **minimum leaf size**, and **`cp`** (complexity parameter) limit how wiggly the tree is.

Split quality can be summarized as impurity decrease:

\[
\Delta I = I(\text{parent}) - p_L I(L) - p_R I(R)
\]

where \(p_L\) and \(p_R\) are child-node proportions.

### `rpart` (Monday genes) в†” `parsnip` (Tuesday penguins)

| Idea | Gene slides / `rpart` | Tuesday `decision_tree` + `rpart` engine |
|------|----------------------|----------------------------------------|
| Depth limit | `maxdepth` in `control` | `tree_depth` |
| Min leaf size | `minsplit` / `minbucket` | `min_n` |
| Pre-prune while growing | `maxdepth`, `minsplit` / `minbucket` | `tree_depth`, `min_n` |
| Post-prune by complexity | `cp` from `$cptable` + `prune()` | usually handled by tuning pre-prune knobs |
| Plot | `rpart.plot`, partition in Gene1Г—Gene3 | `rpart.plot` on fitted workflow |

### Practical `rpart` workflow (`cp` in practice)

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
library(rpart)

fit <- rpart(y ~ ., data = train, method = "class")
printcp(fit)   # table with xerror and xstd
plotcp(fit)    # visual pick of cp region

# choose cp near the 1-SE region (simpler tree with similar error)
cp_star <- fit$cptable[which.min(fit$cptable[, "xerror"]), "CP"]
fit_pruned <- prune(fit, cp = cp_star)
```

- `printcp()` / `plotcp()` help you avoid keeping tiny splits that do not generalize.
- After pruning, compare validation or CV performance to the unpruned fit.

---

## When trees shine or struggle

**Shine:** nonlinear boundaries, interactions without hand-coded products, mixed numeric/categorical inputs after dummy coding.

**Struggle:** very noisy tiny \(n\) without pruning; **high correlation** among splits (importance is not causal вЂ” Thursday VIP).

---

## After Tuesday: same pipeline, new engines

Thursday keeps **`rec_base`** and **`workflow()`** but swaps **`spec`** to forests, boosting, or MLP вЂ” [Module 05](module-05-advanced-models.qmd), [Day 4 Part B](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-b-models).

---

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Day 2 (Tuesday)](../slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd)
- **Notebook:** [penguins-classification.Rmd](../notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd) (`glm` + `rpart`)
- **Pipeline:** [Module 04](module-04-pipeline.qmd)
- **Data:** [Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)
